conversation.text_en: Hi, I'm your therapist. Everything we discuss here is confidential, so please feel comfortable.
Thanks, I appreciate that.
Good to hear the commute was smooth. What brings you in today? Is this a follow-up from previous sessions or something new?
Yeah, I mean, kind of, it's been a while since my last therapy session but... honestly, things just got really tough.
Can you tell me more about what's been going on that feels overwhelming to you right now?
It's like, I just can't get interested in anything anymore. I mean, I used to love driving my truck, even with all the traffic and roadwork, but now it just feels like a chore.
That sounds really tough. How has your sleep been lately?
Sleep fine, actually. Problem is when I wake up. I got this nagging feeling of guilt about something or other that keeps me awake till 3 am thinking about it.
Can you tell me more about what's causing that sense of guilt?
It's just... I feel like I should be doing better, making more progress, achieving my goals. But nothing seems to be working out the way I want it to.
That sounds really frustrating. What are some specific things you're trying to achieve or accomplish right now?
Just feeling stuck, you know? Like, I got my daily routine down, but it's not giving me that sense of fulfillment anymore. And my appetite... forget about it.
How has your appetite been affected?
It's like, severely disrupted. I mean, sometimes I just don't care about eating at all and then other times I'll gorge on junk food thinking it's gonna fill the void or something.
That sounds really challenging. Have you noticed any changes in your energy levels too?
Oh yeah, energy-wise? Forget about it. I'm like a sloth most days. Even simple tasks exhaust me.
How have you been managing your stress and anxiety lately?
Honestly? Not very well. I just try to power through things, but it's like, the more I push, the more exhausted I get and then... boom! Depression hits.
Can you tell me about a recent stressful life event that might have contributed to this feeling of being stuck?
My best friend moved away last month. We'd do these long drives together on the weekends, and now it's just... silence.
That can be really tough to adjust to. What kind of support system do you have around you right now?
Just me, myself, and I. Used to have a buddy or two at work but they're all transferred out now too.
Have you thought about reaching out to anyone in your network for emotional support?
Haven't really, no. Don't wanna burden nobody. Guess I'm just trying to figure this out on my own.
What are some things that give you a sense of joy or fulfillment these days?
Honestly? Nothing comes to mind. Maybe driving... but even that's lost its magic right now.
That sounds really tough. It might be helpful to explore what specifically about driving used to bring you joy and how we can try to rekindle that spark.
Yeah, maybe. I don't know, though. It feels like everything just sucks right now.
Can you tell me more about what you mean by "everything" feeling like it's sucking?
Just... life, work, relationships... nothing seems to be working out the way I want it to.
What are some things that you're hoping to achieve or accomplish in the next few weeks? Any small steps you can take towards getting unstuck?
Honestly? Not sure. Guess just wanting to not feel like this all the time would be a start.
That's definitely a great place to start. What do you think might be one small step towards moving forward for you?
I don't know... maybe try reaching out to some old friends or something? Don't wanna bother them, though.
It sounds like you're willing to consider that as an option. How about we set a goal for the next session to make contact with someone from your past and see how it goes?
Yeah, okay... I guess that's not too bad of an idea.
Great! Let's work on making that happen and then explore some other coping strategies and goals in our next session.
